verse.translation: And indeed, you are of a great moral character.

theme_question.correct: The sublime moral character of Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ)
options: The sublime moral character of Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ)
The obligation of the five daily prayers
The scientific miracles described in the Quran
The historical account of the people of 'Ad

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse was revealed in Makkah as a divine testimony to the Prophet Muhammad's (ﷺ) character, in response to the disbelievers who slandered him. It affirms that his conduct was not something he fabricated but was a reflection of the divine guidance he received. It stands as a powerful defense and an honor from Allah.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The theme is the perfection of the Prophet's (ﷺ) character ('khuluq'). The term 'khuluq 'azim' (great moral character) is comprehensive, encompassing all virtues like patience, mercy, truthfulness, humility, and justice. This verse establishes him as the ultimate role model for humanity, a 'walking Quran' whose life was the practical application of its teachings.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Al-Qalam, named after the pen mentioned in the first verse, is a Makkan surah. It begins by swearing by the pen and what is written, defending the Prophet (ﷺ) against accusations of madness. It contrasts the fate of the believers with that of the deniers, using the parable of the 'Companions of the Garden' to warn against arrogance and ingratitude. The surah concludes by encouraging the Prophet to be patient and steadfast in his mission.
